title: Patterson's 49: the day the Trophy race broke open
title_candidates: Patterson's 49: the day the Trophy race broke open
From triple-triple to Trophy lead: JP's Praia rampage
The second-best round in TEG history
Patterson laps the field at Praia D'El Rey
theme: John Patterson, last seen carding back-to-back triple bogeys at the 5th and 6th, somehow assembles the second-best round in TEG history and turns a one-shot Trophy race into a ten-point procession.
opening_hook: Open on the bare fact: 49 Stableford points, the second-best round in TEG history — then immediately rewind to JP at six over through six, because the day's defining number was nowhere in sight until it suddenly was. The single dominant arc justifies theme_led.
foreshadow: JP's opening horror show — bogey-bogey-bogey-double-triple-triple through six (b18) — set up as the round nobody saw coming
The birdie at the 7th (b12) as the hinge: the moment the bleeding stopped and the rampage began
Jon Baker's three-par run at 11-13 (b17) framed as the leader holding firm — before the 14th tee

competition_state.name: Trophy
competition_state.note: Blown wide open. JP began the day tied for 2nd, one behind Jon Baker; 49 points later he leads by ten. DM briefly drew level on H2 (b23), JP retook level on H11 (b24), and from there it was a solo. JB went from leader to T3.

competition_state.name: Green Jacket
competition_state.note: Mullin held the Jacket lead but extended it from two to seven. JB stays 2nd; JP's gross 14-over was strong enough to keep him in third but not to threaten DM in the net race.

competition_state.name: Wooden Spoon
competition_state.note: SN remains rooted to the bottom and the gap to GW widened from 8 to 14. Two quads (5th, 9th) and a nine-shot bleed at 11-13 ensured the Spoon's grip tightened rather than loosened.

players.arc: From six over through six to a 49-point demolition — back-to-back triples at 5-6, a birdie at the 7th to staunch it, then 17 points across 9-13 and 14 more from 15-18; takes the Trophy lead on H1, loses it briefly, reclaims it level on H11, and finishes ten clear.
Quietly does his Jacket job: weathers a four-bogey patch broken by a birdie at the 10th, briefly drew level on Trophy at H2, and ends the day with his Green Jacket lead stretched from two to seven.

Started the day as Trophy leader and ended it ten back; a three-par procession at 11-13 suggested he might dig in, before the quad at the par-three 14th — alongside Alex — undid the round, only partly salvaged by a birdie at the 17th.

A personal-best 44 points that, on any other day, would have been the headline — achieved despite two quadruple bogeys (at the 10th and the 14th) and a three-hole bleed of eight shots; the 10-point burst at 7-9 was the engine.
Largely anonymous bar a 10 at the par-four 9th — a sextuple bogey that becomes the round's most lurid single number even on a day full of competition.
Two quads (5th and 9th) and a triple-triple-triple at 11-13; the Spoon gap to GW doubles from 8 to 14.

venue_notes: Third TEG visit to Praia D'El Rey (Cabell Robinson, links-and-parkland on the Lisbon Coast); TEG's third trip to the area. The course's dramatic back-nine dunes are the backdrop for JP's 9-13 surge and JB/AB's twin quads at the par-three 14th.
